description: Explore CLI command registry, command docs, tests, and templates to identify all removal points and public surface dependencies.
focus: packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts; CLI README; command tests; package bin/help; references to removed commands
findings: CLI public surface is centralized in packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts. Remove approve/update/sync-meta/human-lint/ledger-append/pre-commit/bootstrap/config/hooks from allCommands to drop public runtime/help, but keep or relocate helper APIs used by init. Docs/templates/tests/i18n still advertise removed commands. Doctor CLI lacks target --json/--strict semantics.

description: Explore doctor, sync-meta, rule-test index, meta generation, and deterministic fix behavior to plan absorbing sync-meta into doctor --fix.
focus: packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts; packages/cli/src/commands/sync-meta.ts; rule-test.index; agents.meta generation; fixable/manual errors
findings: sync-meta currently owns useful algorithms for agents.meta/rule-test.index but is bound to .fabric/agents and writes events itself. Doctor currently mixes forensic/meta/human-lock/ledger/audit/business anchors/rule-test checks and fix runs migrateLegacyLedger + syncExistingMetaBaseline. Plan should extract a rules-based meta/rule-test builder over .fabric/rules content_ref, then have doctor --fix call it and remove human-lock/legacy/audit-completeness checks from core doctor.

description: Explore init outputs, templates, skills, bootstrap docs, and schema usage to migrate target state from .fabric/agents/human-lock/legacy ledger to .fabric/rules/events only.
focus: init output; .fabric/rules; INITIAL_TAXONOMY; forensic; init-context; human-lock; events ledger; no legacy compatibility
findings: Init is not target-aligned: it still plans/writes .fabric/human-lock.json and templates/skills direct rules to .fabric/agents. Target should keep bootstrap, INITIAL_TAXONOMY, forensic, init-context, rules, agents.meta, rule-test.index, events. Event ledger service already writes .fabric/events.jsonl. Docs/templates/dashboard still expose human-lock, approve, sync-meta, .fabric/agents and old ledgers.

description: Explore MCP tool registration, HTTP APIs, event ledger/audit projection, and tests to remove fab_append_intent/fab_update_registry and keep MCP readiness checks in doctor.
focus: packages/server/src/index.ts; tools append-intent/update-registry; event-ledger; audit-log; rule-sections; plan-context; tests
findings: MCP registration still imports/registers fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry in packages/server/src/index.ts; index.test asserts deprecated descriptions. Replacement APIs exist: planContext writes rule_context_planned, getRuleSections writes rule_selection and rule_sections_fetched, HTTP JsonlEventStore writes mcp_event. Removing deprecated tools should delete/update tool/service files and tests; doctor should check event ledger/readiness not legacy audit/ledger projections.
